name: HMS Loch Achray (K 426)
events: 7 Feb 1945 HMS Ultor (Lt. J.P. Harvey, RN) departed Scapa Flow for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR). (1)
24 Feb 1945 HMS Trusty (Lt. H.S. May, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR). (2)

15 Mar 1945 HMS Unruly (T/Lt. D.J. Palmer, RNVR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN) and HMS Aire (A/Lt.Cdr. L.A., DSC, RNR). (3)

16 Mar 1945 HMS Unruly (T/Lt. D.J. Palmer, RNVR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR) and later with HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN) and HMS Aire (A/Lt.Cdr. L.A., DSC, RNR). (3)

17 Mar 1945 HMS Unruly (T/Lt. D.J. Palmer, RNVR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RD, RNR) and HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR) and later with HMS Liberty (T/Cdr. J.S. Roe, DSC, RNVR) and HMS Mounsey (?). (3)

22 Mar 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Mounsey (?) and HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN). Also a practice attack was carried out during which HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR) served as the target. (4)
23 Mar 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Liberty (T/Cdr. J.S. Roe, DSC, RNVR) and HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR). (4)

7 Apr 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board) and HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RD, RNR). (4)

7 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (5)

8 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (6)

9 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (5)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
